initial_program:
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Cells are exactly the coordinates that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Grid size  the highest row and column indices that occur
max_row(MR) :- MR = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col(MC) :- MC = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% 4connected neighbour offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Adjacent cells  used for the floodfill
adjacent(Rp,Cp,R,C) :-
    delta(DR,DC),
    R = Rp + DR,
    C = Cp + DC,
    cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,V),
    V != 3.

% Boundary cells  cells on the outer rim that are not colour3
boundary(R,C) :- cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3, R = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3, C = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3, R = MR.
boundary(R,C) :- cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3, C = MC.

% Floodfill from the boundary through cells that are not colour3
outside(R,C) :- boundary(R,C).
outside(R,C) :- outside(Rp,Cp), adjacent(Rp,Cp,R,C),
                input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Cells that are not reachable from the boundary and are not colour3
interior(R,C) :- cell(R,C), not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Allowed output colours for each cell
allowed(R,C,3) :- input(R,C,3).                                   % colour3 stays 3
allowed(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, outside(R,C).             % reachable cells keep their colour (V3)
allowed(R,C,4) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0).                  % interior zero cells become 4
allowed(R,C,V) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3.   % interior nonzero cells keep colour

% Exactly one colour for each cell, chosen from the allowed set
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(0..9), allowed(R,C,Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

rounds.program_after:
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Cells are exactly the coordinates that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Grid size  the highest row and column indices that occur
max_row(MR) :- MR = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col(MC) :- MC = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% 4connected neighbour offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Adjacent cells  used for the floodfill
adjacent(Rp,Cp,R,C) :-
    delta(DR,DC),
    R = Rp + DR,
    C = Cp + DC,
    cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,V),
    V != 3.

% Boundary cells  cells on the outer rim that are not colour3
boundary(R,C) :- cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3, R = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3, C = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3, R = MR.
boundary(R,C) :- cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3, C = MC.

% Floodfill from the boundary through cells that are not colour3
outside(R,C) :- boundary(R,C).
outside(R,C) :- outside(Rp,Cp), adjacent(Rp,Cp,R,C),
                input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Cells that are not reachable from the boundary and are not colour3
interior(R,C) :- cell(R,C), not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Allowed output colours for each cell
allowed(R,C,3) :- input(R,C,3).                                   % colour3 stays 3
allowed(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, outside(R,C).             % reachable cells keep their colour (V3)
allowed(R,C,4) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0).                  % interior zero cells become 4
allowed(R,C,V) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3.   % interior nonzero cells keep colour

% Exactly one colour for each cell, chosen from the allowed set
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(0..9), allowed(R,C,Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

final_program:
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Cells are exactly the coordinates that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Grid size  the highest row and column indices that occur
max_row(MR) :- MR = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_col(MC) :- MC = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% 4connected neighbour offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Adjacent cells  used for the floodfill
adjacent(Rp,Cp,R,C) :-
    delta(DR,DC),
    R = Rp + DR,
    C = Cp + DC,
    cell(R,C),
    input(R,C,V),
    V != 3.

% Boundary cells  cells on the outer rim that are not colour3
boundary(R,C) :- cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3, R = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3, C = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3, R = MR.
boundary(R,C) :- cell(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3, C = MC.

% Floodfill from the boundary through cells that are not colour3
outside(R,C) :- boundary(R,C).
outside(R,C) :- outside(Rp,Cp), adjacent(Rp,Cp,R,C),
                input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Cells that are not reachable from the boundary and are not colour3
interior(R,C) :- cell(R,C), not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Allowed output colours for each cell
allowed(R,C,3) :- input(R,C,3).                                   % colour3 stays 3
allowed(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, outside(R,C).             % reachable cells keep their colour (V3)
allowed(R,C,4) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0).                  % interior zero cells become 4
allowed(R,C,V) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3.   % interior nonzero cells keep colour

% Exactly one colour for each cell, chosen from the allowed set
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(0..9), allowed(R,C,Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).
